contact_lookup_status: Named contact Lucas Fernandes was verified from the public LinkedIn posting checked on 2026-04-12.
why_company: What caught my attention about appliedAI is that this role is not about AI as branding. It is about helping people actually learn it: updating content, structuring learning materials, scaling programs, and making complex topics accessible. That fits a side of my work that has become much more real over the past year through both hands-on AI workflow building and my public writing about AI on pmlecuong.com.
job_description: appliedAI Initiative GmbH is hiring a Working Student (m/f/x) in Instructional Design in Heilbronn, with hybrid work also linked to Munich. The role supports Instructional Designers in updating and translating content using tools such as Articulate, Moodle, and Area9, uploading learning materials to LMS platforms, improving and scaling educational offerings, standardizing programs, and contributing ideas and feedback for high-quality AI learning experiences. The posting asks for students in Psychology, Education, Instructional Design, or a related field, a genuine interest in education and e-learning, excitement to learn new platforms, strong communication skills, independent organization, and fluent German and English.
requirements: Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's program in Psychology, Education, Instructional Design, or a related field
Genuine interest in education, e-learning, and how people learn
First experience in instructional design or educational projects is a plus
Willingness to learn tools and platforms such as Moodle or Area9
Strong communication skills and ability to organize tasks independently
Interest in making AI learning accessible and scalable
cv_about_override: Business analysis and AI workflow professional focused on structured communication, operational clarity, and turning complex technical topics into practical workflows and usable knowledge.
cv_summary_override: Experienced business analysis and AI workflow professional with a strong foundation in structured execution, stakeholder alignment, workflow improvement, and practical communication across technical and non-technical contexts. Alongside my earlier product and business-analysis work, I have been building a stronger AI-focused profile through Software Engineering studies, hands-on automation and agent workflows, and public writing that explains complex AI topics in a more usable and accessible way. This makes me well suited for working student roles that combine AI, structured content, learning enablement, and practical support for educational programs.
cover_letter_opening: I wanted to apply for the Working Student (m/f/x) - Instructional Design role at appliedAI because the work itself caught my attention immediately: helping people understand AI better through structured content and scalable learning materials.
cover_letter_body_one: My background is not only in product or business analysis. Over the past year, I have written extensively on pmlecuong.com about AI agents, context engineering, and practical AI adoption. That pushed me to translate technical ideas into clearer language, structure, and examples that other people can actually use.
cover_letter_body_two: That is why this role feels like a real fit. Through my Software Engineering studies at Heilbronn University, my Business Information Systems background, and my hands-on work across automation, AI workflows, and documentation, I have been moving toward work that sits between technical depth and human understanding. I do not come from a classic instructional-design background, but I learn quickly, write clearly, and care about making complex topics easier to navigate.
cover_letter_motivation: I am particularly interested in appliedAI because the mission is not just to talk about AI, but to help professionals learn and apply it in a practical way. That is very close to how I already think and write about AI.
cover_letter_closing: @@AVAILABILITY@@. I also want to be transparent that the posting asks for fluent German, while my German is still improving and not yet at that level. I work confidently in English at C1 level with IELTS 7.5 and continue to strengthen my German. If another suitable English-first working student role at appliedAI is closer to my current language level, I would also be grateful to be considered.
